explicit_text: I was born in Burma in 1993, and my ethnicity is Karen. Although I left Burma at a young age, it is my birthplace and a part of my identity. My early life began there before my family moved to a refugee camp in Thailand.
implicit_text: I was born in a Southeast Asian country in 1993, and my ethnicity is Karen. Although I left that country at a young age, it is my birthplace and a part of my identity. My early life began there before my family moved to a refugee camp in Thailand.

explicit_text: I grew up in a Thai refugee camp in Chai Mai, Thailand, where I lived for ten years. Life was hard there, and making a living was not easy, so education was very important to us. I attended a Thai middle school with my best friend Wah Paw, and we shared many experiences, including getting punished for wearing our Karen traditional bags.

explicit_text: My family moved to the United States in 2007, starting our new life in Phoenix, Arizona. Later, we relocated to Saint Paul, Minnesota, where I continued my education and graduated from high school. Now, I am a student at Saint Paul College, and I feel proud of how far I have come since arriving in the U.S.

explicit_text: After my sophomore year, my family moved to Saint Paul, Minnesota, where I finished high school at Como Park Senior High School. Saint Paul became my new home, and I continued to grow and learn there. Now, I am attending Saint Paul College, which makes me feel connected to this city.
